chunk_summary: Balanced growth assessment post-discharge for preterm/LBW/ELBW infants involves monitoring weight, length, and head circumference trajectories to ensure proportional development and avoid risks like overnutrition or inadequate brain growth.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of balanced growth assessment using weight, length, and head circumference post-discharge in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants.
Identify signs of growth faltering, including downward crossing of z-scores or percentile lines.
Learn strategies to prevent disproportionate catch-up growth and overnutrition risks in preterm infants.
key_facts: Weight-only success can mask poor lean mass accretion, delayed linear growth, or inadequate brain growth.
AAP defines growth faltering risk as weight-for-age or weight-for-length falling very low or crossing two major percentile lines downward.
Head circumference is a proxy for brain growth; inadequate growth signals protein and intake concerns.
Weight-for-length >85th percentile is a trigger for revising supplementation per ABM protocol.
Excessive rapid catch-up growth increases later metabolic risk, emphasizing balanced catch-up over maximal weight gain.
reference_quote: The ABM protocol uses >85th percentile weight-for-length as a trigger for revising supplementation, especially if length gain is lagging.
document_key_topics: Growth faltering risk and z-score monitoring
